text.title: topical-authority Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for topical-authority. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Structure content as topic clusters: one pillar page (broad overview of the topic) supported by 8-30 cluster articles (specific subtopics), with bidirectional internal links between them.
rule.rationale: Google's helpful-content and semantic-relevance systems assess topical coverage, not just individual keyword optimisation. A cluster of 10-30 interlinking articles on RAG signals deeper expertise than a single article — even one highly optimised for the target keyword. AI Overviews preferentially cite recognised topical authorities.

rule.statement: Answer all related questions, cover edge cases, and refresh content at least every 6 months — comprehensive coverage signals topical expertise more than article length alone.
rule.rationale: Google's helpful-content evaluation rewards content that fully satisfies user intent. Thin cluster articles that repeat what the pillar already covers dilute rather than amplify authority.

rule.statement: Link from pillar to all cluster articles; link from each cluster article back to the pillar and to 2-4 related clusters; use keyword-rich anchor text that describes the destination content.
rule.rationale: Internal linking distributes PageRank across the cluster and signals to Google that these pages form a related content group. Anchor text reinforces keyword relevance without the manipulation risk of external anchor text over-optimisation.
